title: Brand & Content Designer (Remote, Full-Time)
description:
Job description



At Miraco Nutripharm, we are dedicated to advancing health and improving lives. Our goal is to relieve 1 billion people worldwide of pain and discomfort. If you know of anyone who has been worried about or suffered from any health problems, join us in making a positive impact by reducing the pain and distress others have to endure.



We’re seeking a full-time Brand and Content Designer to lead the creative direction of our brand across packaging, offline marketing, and digital platforms. This role is ideal for a strategic, hands-on creative who can own both design and content production, from ideation to execution, while supporting our expansion across Southeast Asia and global markets.



As a key member of the team, you'll be responsible for producing compelling visuals and content that reflect the heart of our brand and connect meaningfully with consumers, healthcare professionals, and distribution partners.



Key Responsibilities:



-Brand Development & Creative Direction-

*Lead development of visual identity for new and existing brands, including logos, packaging, brand guidelines, typography and brand story

*Propose and implement creative directions from Pantone color palettes to iconography, font systems and photography guidelines

*Establish and maintain consistent branding across all physical and digital touchpoints, while ensuring all brand assets are stored and updated in an organized, easily accessible database



-Graphic & Marketing Design-

*Design high-impact materials such as product packaging, brochures, point-of-sale displays, in-store visuals and Continuing Medical Education (CME) event collaterals

*Develop pitch decks, sales presentations and brand kits for internal and external stakeholders

*Create visuals for promotions, campaigns, and trade shows for greater awareness and conversion



-Content Creation & Copywriting-

*Collaborate with the team to develop content ideas and messaging for various platforms (digital ads, social media, web,
)

*Write and edit marketing copy as needed, including taglines, brand messaging, product descriptions, and promotional materials

*Contribute to storytelling, campaign ideation, consumer education initiatives, and customer loyalty initiatives



-Digital & Multimedia Content-

*Produce digital assets for ecommerce platforms, social media ads, newsletters, and display ads (Google Ads Meta, TikTok)

*Edit videos and animations for campaigns, tutorials, events, and educational content using tools like CapCut, Premiere Pro, or After Effects

*Repurpose content across multiple formats and languages when applicable



-Cross-Functional Collaboration-

*Work closely with marketing, sales, pharmacists and leadership to turn strategic goals into compelling creative output

*Participate in product launch discussions and brand development brainstorming sessions

*Coordinate with external freelancers or vendors as needed



-Regional Support & Market Expansion-

*Adapt assets for regional markets including Singapore, Malaysia, Mainland China & Hong Kong and future expansion areas

*Ensure consistency across regions while accounting for local cultural, language, market and regulatory differences



-Requirements-

*Minimum 3–5 years of experience in graphic design, brand development, content creation, or multimedia production

*Strong portfolio showcasing design, branding, and content/copywriting capabilities across print and digital

*Proficient in Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, Photoshop, Premiere Pro), CapCut, Canva, Figma, or similar tools

*Excellent understanding of branding, packaging, digital marketing, and customer communication

*Creative thinker with strong attention to detail and an eye for aesthetic balance

*Highly organized, self-driven, and able to manage multiple timelines in a fast-paced environment

*Strong communication and collaboration skills, especially in remote work settings

*Experience in the health, wellness, pharmaceutical, or consumer goods sector is a plus

*Degree in Design, Marketing, Communications, or a related field preferred but not mandatory



Kindly submit your updated CV and sample portfolio to 
 for our review.

title: Senior Executive Assistant to the CEO (Full-Time, Dedicated Role)
description:
BLUEFLOWER LTD.

Blueflower is a global travel design company crafting intelligent, highly personalized journeys. We design meaningful travel experiences with structure, precision, and care. Our team works remotely across countries and time zones, united by a commitment to excellence and elegant execution.



The Role

We are hiring a Senior Executive Assistant to the CEO. This is a full-time, exclusive role working directly with the CEO of an international company.



The role requires judgment, structure, and independence. You will act as a close partner to the CEO, ensuring priorities are managed, information is organized, and execution keeps pace with decision-making. Due to the level of trust required, this role demands full professional focus and availability.



This is not an administrative, VA, or task-only role.



Responsibilities

- Manage the CEO’s calendar across time zones and ensure meeting readiness

- Support executive decision-making through organization, tracking, and follow-through

- Own inbox outcomes by surfacing priorities and ensuring timely responses

- Act as a reminder and filter so key items do not fall through the cracks

- Manage executive workflows with clients, internal teams, and partners

- Own select internal projects and follow up on assigned action items

- Ensure internal standards and preparation requirements are met

- Oversee executive travel readiness with end-to-end ownership and foresight



Requirements

- 7–10 years of experience as an Executive Assistant, ideally supporting a C-level executive

- Direct experience supporting a non-Filipino executive in an international or multicultural environment

- Strong written and spoken English with a professional business tone

- Ability to manage senior-level priorities rather than task execution alone

- High level of discretion, reliability, and judgment

- Comfortable working with imperfect information and evolving priorities

- Proactive, calm, and highly organized

- Able to commit to a full-time role with no other concurrent jobs or long-term clients



Work Setup

Remote, full-time role (40+ hours per week)

Exclusive engagement. This role is not compatible with holding other jobs or freelance work

Long-term position

Work closely with a globally distributed team



How to Apply

Send your application with:

1. Personalized CV

2. Tailored cover letter

Before applying, review www.blueflower.la

In your cover letter, please answer the following:

- What stands out to you about Blueflower after reviewing our website

- Why you are specifically interested in this role

- The executive(s) you have supported and in what capacity



Incomplete or non-personalized applications will be automatically disqualified.

title: Sales Manager
description:
The Everyday Cleaners, Seattle, WA. YOUR JOB HUNT STOPS HERE!

Looking to hire ASAP!



We want someone who is:



-Hungry for growth

-High-energy and proactive

-Obsessed with getting results

-Comfortable moving fast and figuring things out

-If you want something easy and predictable, this isn’t it.

-If you want to level up, take ownership, and be part of building something real, keep reading…





So Who Are We?



We’re The Everyday Cleaners—a growing residential cleaning company based in Seattle Washington!

We don’t clean homes ourselves—we build the machine behind it.

We connect customers with reliable cleaners, manage the entire experience, and make sure every job runs smoothly from booking to completion. Think of us as the operations and sales engine that keeps everything moving.

We’re growing quickly and looking for our next A+ tea
mber—someone who’s NOT average, NOT passive, and NOT here to just “do a job.”





Who are we looking for?



We’re looking for a Sales Manager who is Driven, Competitive, and Obsessed with Winning!



This is NOT a role for someone who just wants a job—we want someone with REAL ambition.

Someone who has that “DAWG” in them. You’re hungry, you bring energy every day, and you don’t wait to be told what to do—you make things happen.



The ideal candidate has experience in Sales, preferably within the Cleaning Industry, and is comfortable using tools like BookingKoala (BK), QuoteIQ (Quo), Slack, and Notion. Strong English and sharp communication skills are a must—you know how to control conversations, build trust, and close deals.



You should be goal-oriented and performance-driven, always pushing for better results and never settling for average. We value people who go above and beyond, take ownership, and look for ways to improve systems, processes, and outcomes.

This role is an opportunity to grow fast, sharpen your skills, and be part of a team that actually cares about performance. We’re looking for someone who brings intensity, executes consistently, and adds real value—someone I can learn from as well.





Why Should You Work Here?



This is not a “stable, slow, corporate job.”

This is an opportunity to get in early—before things scale—and be part of building something that’s growing fast.

Right now, we’re small, which means:

You’ll work directly with decision-makers

Your ideas actually matter and get implemented

You can grow as fast as you can prove yourself

As we grow, top performers don’t stay in the same role—they move up into more responsibility, higher pay, and leadership opportunities.





Compensation:



-5 hours per day

-20 hours per week (Wednesday–Sunday, PST)

-$100 USD per week

-Performance-based bonuses for closed deals





Responsibilities



-Close inbound leads and convert them into booked jobs

-Build and improve sales scripts, processes, and workflows

-Generate new business through outbound outreach (cold calls, partnerships, referrals)

-Ensure fast response times—speed is critical in closing deals

-Set accurate pricing and provide profitable quotes based on job scope

-Track performance, manage the CRM, and hit weekly/monthly sales targets

-Continuously test and improve sales strategies to increase revenue





Ideal Requirements:



-Prior experience in Sales for a Cleaning Company.

-Has that DAWG in them, has lots of energy and is excited for the opportunity.

-Strong communication and organizational skills.

-Stable internet connection and reliable laptop/PC

-Someone who goes above and beyond.

-Strong English.





How to Apply:



Please submit:

-A short message explaining why you believe you would be a strong fit for this Sales Manager role, keep it concise and specific.

-Your updated resume

-Applications without a message will not be considered.



Looking forward to connect! 



Best,

John

title: Digital Marketing Campaign Manager (Australia)
description:
Australian Agency | Full-Time Remote | Long-Term | Above-Market Pay



A growing Melbourne digital marketing agency is hiring a full-time Digital Marketing Campaign Manager to join the team remotely from the Philippines. You will own client campaigns end-to-end across SEO, Google Ads, and social

media — from strategy through to execution, optimisation, and reporting.



This is a long-term, stable role with a clear path to grow into a team lead position as the agency expands.





WHAT YOU WILL DO



• Plan, launch, and optimise campaigns across SEO, Google Ads, and social (paid and organic —
, Instagram, LinkedIn)

• Run Google Ads end-to-end: keyword research, ad copy, targeting, bidding

• Execute SEO strategy: technical audits, on-page optimisation, link building

• Manage social accounts: content calendars, community engagement, paid boosts

• Build client reports in Looker Studio (Data Studio) with GA4, GTM, SEMrush, and Ahrefs data — show ROI, traffic, conversions, and growth

• Sit in on client calls, help shape strategy presentations, and communicate campaign performance clearly in writing and on video





WHAT WE NEED FROM YOU



• 3+ years hands-on digital marketing, ideally in an agency managing multiple client accounts at once

• Strong working knowledge of: Google Ads, GA4, Google Tag Manager, Looker Studio, SEMrush or Ahrefs, Meta Ads Manager, and a social scheduling tool (Hootsuite, Buffer, Later, etc.)

• Able to plan a campaign and explain the strategy — not just execute tasks

• Strong written English for reports, proposals, and ad copy

• Self-motivated, organised, able to own outcomes without being micromanaged

• Reliable internet and a quiet, professional home workspace



Nice to have: WordPress, Canva,
 marketing platforms, Meta ads compliance knowledge (including 18+ categories), and any graphic design or copywriting experience. These are bonuses, not requirements — the agency has a design team.





WHO YOU ARE



• A self-starter who spots what needs doing and does it

• A campaign thinker who understands how paid, organic, and SEO fit together

• Detail-focused — you catch data errors and take pride in accurate reporting

• A clear communicator who can explain strategy to a non-technical client

• Curious — you follow platform changes and industry trends

• Growth-minded — you want to develop and take on more responsibility





WORK ENVIRONMENT



• Full-time remote, Monday to Friday

• Work hours aligned to Melbourne, Australia business hours

• Communication by
, chat, and video

• Direct interaction with the agency director and marketing team

• Working across multiple client accounts simultaneously

• Sole employment — this is not a side job





WHAT WE OFFER



• Above-market pay for the right person (negotiable, based on experience)

• Long-term, stable full-time role with a growing agency

• A clear path to grow into a team coordinator or leadership role

• Exposure to a diverse client portfolio across multiple industries

• A supportive team that values initiative, skill, and results





HOW TO APPLY



Apply only through our application form here (do not
):





The form will ask you to upload your resume, share a short campaign example, and answer a few quick questions about your experience and work setup.



Applications sent any other way will not be reviewed.

title: Startup Fleet Operations & Growth Operator | Private Car Rental (Remote)
description:
Employment Type: Part-Time (leading to Full-Time)



About the Business:

We are building a private vehicle rental operation that specializes in placing reliable economy vehicles with gig economy drivers on platforms like Uber, Lyft, and DoorDash.

This business is at the ground floor. We have one vehicle, an LLC, and a clear goal to scale to five vehicles in 90 days. There is no existing system, no active client base, and no lead flow yet. We are building that from scratch, and that is exactly why this role exists.



This is not a maintenance job. This is a build job. If you need everything already organized before you can be useful, this is not the right fit.



What This Role Actually Is:

You are the person who helps us go from one car to a real operating business. That means creating structure where there is none, owning driver communication, keeping payment enforcement tight, building a renter pipeline from zero, and helping us move fast without building a mess.

You will not be handed a playbook. You will help write it.



What You Will Own:

You will build and maintain a master tracker covering driver info, vehicle status, payment due dates, days late, GPS notes, risk level, and backup driver pipeline. You will own all driver communication including payment reminders, enforcement messages, and onboarding coordination. You will monitor GPS check-ins and flag anything that needs attention. You will help build the first simple SOPs for onboarding, late payments, and driver offboarding. You will help generate the first renter leads using
 Marketplace, local driver groups, and WhatsApp outreach. You will create a backup driver waitlist so the car is never sitting idle waiting for the next qualified renter.



What I Need From You:

You think in order. You know what to build first and what to leave alone until later. You can enforce payment timelines firmly and professionally without turning it into a conflict. You document everything. You take ownership of your lane without needing a manager standing over your shoulder. You understand that at this stage, one unpaid driver or one idle week has a real impact on the business.



Experience That Helps:

Direct experience is not required, but strong preference goes to anyone who has worked in private car rentals, Turo support, HyreCar, rideshare fleet coordination, driver operations, payment enforcement, lead generation for local service businesses,
 Marketplace listings, or remote operational coordination. You should be comfortable in Google Sheets. If you have used GPS tracking systems or fleet management tools, tell us exactly which ones and what you did with them.



Hours and Pay:

20 hours per week to start. Must be available during CST business hours for overlap. Pay is $5 to $7.50 per hour depending on experience, execution quality, and fit.



How to Apply:

Use the subject line exactly as written: The Sauce: Fleet Operator

Then send all five of the following. Applications missing any of them will not be reviewed.

One, a short summary of your experience in rentals, driver support, operations, or lead generation. Be specific. Do not write general VA experience.



Two, the exact tools you have personally used for tracking, GPS monitoring, CRM, fleet management, or lead handling. Name them and tell us what you did with them.

Three, your answer to this scenario: A driver is two days late on their weekly payment. They are still responding but keep saying tomorrow. The GPS shows the car is active daily. What do you do from this 
nt until it is resolved? Give me the full timeline, what you say, how you document it, and when the kill switch becomes part of the equation.



Four, your answer to this build question: If you stepped into a one car rental business with no lead system, no landing page, and no active clients yet, what are the first five things you build in week one and why? Keep it simple and realistic for this stage.

Five, a two to three minute Loom video explaining why you are the right fit for this specific stage of the business, not fleet management in general.

title: DevOps/Senior Full Stack Developer
description:
We are on the hunt for a DevOps/Senior Full Stack Developer who embodies technical prowess and thrives in an environment where hard work, direct communication, and good humour are a staple of our day. If you’re ready to contribute to a team that pushes the envelope while rowing towards a common mission, you might just be the ideal candidate.



In this role, your responsibilities will span several critical technology domains:

1. API and Automation Development: You will be responsible for maintaining and building our internal APIs and automations, ensuring optimal functionality and adaptability to meet our needs. You will also work on our internal tools.

Customer Integration Support: You will assist our customer-facing team with integrating our solutions into client CRMs and other APIs, facilitating efficient and seamless interactions.

2. You will be responsible for improving landing page performance through ongoing visual updates and structured A/B testing. This includes testing headlines, layouts, and user flows to increase conversion rates, analysing results, and implementing winning variations. You’ll work closely with marketing to ensure all pages are optimised for performance, user experience, and lead quality.

3. Software Maintenance: Working alongside our COO, you will help maintain and enhance our internal proprietary lead distribution software, which is pivotal for managing lead capture, validation and distribution to client APIs. This software acts as the business OS.

4. Innovation and Creative Development: Innovation is at our core. You’ll have the chance to work with cutting-edge technologies, including AI, and engage in ambitious projects where you can unleash your creative potential and have a significant hand in their development from start to finish.



About You:

Experience: You should have at least 5 years of robust experience in full-stack development, with strong expertise in Node.js, Express, and SQL databases. Proficiency in JavaScript and API’s is essential, Experience with Zapier, N8N and CRM’s (Mautic, Pipedrive etc..), Next JS, Express JS - For Backend, Vanilla JS, CSS, Figma, Framer, GIT version control commands. Python and React(MUI) knowledge is also good to have but not required.

Industry Knowledge: A background in marketing technologies and CRM systems is highly favoured, enabling you to effectively support our bespoke needs and customer integrations.

Work Ethic: You are known for your drive, diligence, and commitment to excellence, thriving in an environment free of BS and big egos, ready to face challenges head-on.

Culture Fit: You appreciate a team-oriented atmosphere where everyone contributes and maintains a spirited demeanour, even under pressure. You are dedicated to our collective mission and ready to make a positive impact on our team dynamics.



How to Apply:

If you’re excited about making a substantial impact and pushing the boundaries of technology with a team that blends hard work with fun, we want to hear from you. Send us your CV and a cover letter explaining why you’re the perfect fit for this role. During the application process, we will require you to verify your identity to maintain the authenticity of our hiring process.

Filipino candidate ONLY

Strong background in inventory forecasting and stock management

Experience coordinating with China-based suppliers

Highly organized and detail-oriented

Proactive mindset, you don’t wait, you act

Fast thinker with strong problem-solving skills

RESPONSIBILITIES

Present a clear execution plan for the following week



If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, staying ahead, and keeping things running smoothly, this role is for you.



Excited to see your application! Make sure to provide your resume link.

title: EBAY / POSHMARK LISTING & MANAGING SUPERSTAR WANTED
description:
Part-Time Virtual Assistant

eBay / Poshmark Fashion Accessories Seller 

20 hours/week • Remote • Long-term



About This Role

I run a growing eBay store in the USA specializing in fashion accessories and I am looking for a reliable, detail-oriented VA based in the Philippines to take over the day-to-day selling tasks listed below. This is a part-time role with room to grow into full-time as the business scales. I want someone who can work independently, communicate proactively, and treat this store like their own.



Quick Facts

• Hours: 20 hours per week to start, flexible scheduling

• Pay: Negotiable, commensurate with experience

• How to apply: Onlinejobs.ph message or

• Duration: Long-term preferred — I am not looking for a one-off hire

• Tools used: eBay Seller Hub, Poshmark, Depop, Google Sheets, basic



Your 7 Core Tasks

The table below describes everything you will be responsible for. All tasks are done inside eBay Seller Hub, Poshmark, Depop, or Google Sheets — no special software required.



# Task What it involves Frequency

1 Relisting items Check sold orders from the past 30 days that have positive feedback. Create draft listings in Seller Hub using Sell Similar for high-velocity SKUs. Maintain a stock of 10 drafts per top SKU so the owner can publish quickly from mobile. Daily / Weekly

2 Customer service Monitor and respond to buyer messages in eBay Seller Hub. Handle refund requests professionally and follow eBay Money Back Guarantee policy. Escalate unusual cases to the owner. Daily

3 Dispute monitoring Check the Resolution Centre daily for any open disputes or cases. Respond promptly with tracking information, photos, or other evidence. Keep a log of all disputes and outcomes in Google Sheets. Daily

4 Negative feedback removal Identify any new negative or neutral feedback. Attempt to resolve the buyer's issue and request feedback revision where appropriate. Follow eBay's feedback revision process correctly. As needed

5 eBay seller health Check the Seller Dashboard weekly for any defect rate warnings, late shipment flags, or policy alerts. Report any issues to the owner immediately with a brief summary. Weekly

6 Crosslisting List active eBay fashion accessories inventory on Poshmark and Depop. Adapt titles and descriptions to suit each platform’s audience and SEO. Keep pricing consistent across platforms. Monitoring Shipstation Inventory prior to listing.

7 Blocking buyers After owner approval or a clear policy violation, add problematic buyers to the Blocked Buyer List in eBay Account Settings. Keep a Google Sheet log of blocked buyers with reason and date. As needed





What I Am Looking For

Must Have

• Proven experience listing and managing an eBay seller account (Seller Hub, not just buying)

• Familiarity with eBay policies: Money Back Guarantee, defect rates, feedback removal

• Experience with Poshmark and/or Depop listing

• Comfortable writing professional customer service replies in English

• Reliable internet connection and available during agreed hours

• Able to work independently with minimal supervision



Nice to Have

• Experience with fashion accessories, jewelry, belts, hats or similar categories

• Google Sheets experience for logging and tracking

• Familiarity with eBay’s Cassini search algorithm and listing optimization

• Experience handling eBay disputes and escalations

• Experience solving problems with ChatGBT or Claude



What I Offer

• Consistent part-time income with potential to grow to full-time

• Clear task list with no guessing— you will always know what to do

• Respectful, straightforward working relationship

• Paid training period to learn my specific workflow

• Long-term opportunity as the business scales



How to Apply

Please send me a message that includes ALL of the following. Applications missing any item will not be considered. 



• A short introduction about yourself (3–5 sentences)

• How many years you have managed eBay seller accounts and what categories you sold

• Your experience with Poshmark and/or Depop

• One example of how you handled a difficult buyer or dispute

• Your available hours per week and preferred schedule (Philippine time)

• Your rate in USD per hour

• 

This position requires close attention to detail please respond with "Kent I am Your eBay Expert!" in the subject line of your response to this job posting.  I look forward to hearing from candidates who have read this post carefully.





Thank you for your interest — I look forward to finding the right long-term partner for this role.

mbers, offshore and onshore, vendors and clients

title: AI ART DESIGNER
description:
Description:

We are building a digital art brand focused on high-quality artwork that meets certain specifications. We need an AI Art Designer who can consistently produce polished, style-specific images—not random outputs.



This role is production-focused with clear standards and ongoing work for the right person.



Responsibilities:

Generate AI artwork (landscapes, interiors, seasonal, traditional styles)

Match specific styles (e.g., oil painting with visible brush texture, realistic lighting)

Follow exact requirements:

3840 × 2160 resolution (16:9)

No distortion or stretching

Clean composition

Refine prompts and iterate based on feedback

Deliver organized, high-quality files



Requirements:

Experience with Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, or similar tools

Strong visual judgment (composition, lighting, realism)

Ability to replicate a consistent style

Attention to detail (no artifacts, warped structures, etc.)

Reliable and responsive



Pay:

$3–$4/hour depending on experience

Long-term opportunity with consistent work



To Apply:

Share 3–5 samples of AI-generated images you’ve created

List the tools you used



Selected candidates will be asked to do a paid test requiring the following:



Part 1 – Style Matching (Realism / Painterly)



Create an image based on:



Christ walking out of the tomb, backlit with warm light, wearing flowing white robes, emerging into a bright landscape.

Style should be inspired by traditional Christian painters (e.g., soft, reverent, painterly realism, similar feel to artists like Roger Loveless or Yongsung Kim).

Focus on lighting, emotion, and realism.

Composition must be 16:9.



Part 2 – Style Transformation (Same Scene, New Style)



Create the same scene, but in a completely different style:



Light, airy watercolor style

Soft edges, minimal detail, bright and uplifting tone

Emphasis on atmosphere over realism



Part 3 – Process Explanation



Briefly explain:



What tools you used

How you adjusted prompts between the two styles

Any editing or upscaling process

What We’re Evaluating

Ability to match a specific artistic style

Ability to intentionally change styles while keeping the same scene

Composition and lighting quality

Attention to detail (no distortions, clean structure)



Do NOT Apply If:

You rely on generic prompts without control

You cannot recreate a consistent look

You submit unedited or low-quality outputs



Bonus:

Experience with religious or traditional artwork

Strong understanding of painting styles (oil vs watercolor, etc.)

Experience creating artwork for print or display

title: Experienced Project Manager (Amazon FBA | Full-Time)
description:
Join a Fast-Growing E-Commerce Brand Based in Singapore



We’re a fast-growing Singapore-based Amazon FBA brand, and we’re looking for a strong Amazon Project Manager to help us scale.



This is not a coordinator role.

You’ll be managing people, systems, and execution across our Amazon operations.



We’re looking for someone who already knows the Amazon backend and can operate independently without being hand-held.



What you’ll be doing



You’ll manage a team of around 6 people across listings, design, and operations.



You’ll coordinate shipping plans with suppliers, freight forwarders, and inspection agents.

You’ll review and improve product listings, making sure keywords, images, and backend data are correct.

You’ll track inventory, sales, and campaigns, and keep our spreadsheets and dashboards accurate.

You’ll work closely with our design, listing, and ops teams to keep launches and updates on track.



You’ll take part in internal training, including AI-powered workflows.

AI is a tool, not a crutch. We expect you to use it to think better, work faster, and improve outcomes, not to blindly paste output.



You’ll help plan projects, improve processes, and make sure details like product size tiers and listing data are correct before they cost us money.



Tools you’ll use



Amazon Seller Central

Google Sheets and Docs

Notion

Helium 10

DataDive

Zoom



What we’re looking for



At least 5 years of relevant experience, ideally in Amazon FBA or e-commerce operations.

Real experience with Amazon listings, inventory tracking, and backend workflows.

Strong attention to detail and the ability to communicate clearly with different teams.

Able to work independently, prioritise well, and adapt when things change.

Strong problem-solving, coordination, and analytical skills.

Proactive, open-minded, and able to work well in a fast-moving team.



Working hours



Monday to Friday, 12 PM to 10 PM Singapore time

1 hour break

Remote, 44 hours per week

A short introduction about yourself and why you’re a good fit

Clear details of your Amazon FBA experience



Important



Only candidates with real Amazon FBA experience will be considered.

Applications without references or relevant background will be ignored.

If you are new to Amazon or need training, this is not the right role for you.

title: Senior Teramind Specialist (Remote)
description:
We are seeking a senior-level Teramind Specialist to support ongoing operations and project-based deployments across our business.

This role is suited to an experienced professional who can operate autonomously and manage the Teramind platform end-to-end with confidence and precision.



Role Overview

You will be responsible for the deployment, configuration, and ongoing management of Teramind, ensuring it is implemented effectively to support operational visibility, compliance, and user activity monitoring.

This is a remote contractor role with flexible working arrangements, with availability aligned to Australian (AEST) business hours where required.



Key Responsibilities

• Deploy and configure Teramind across environments

• Design, implement, and maintain monitoring policies

• Configure and manage user activity tracking and controls

• Ensure accurate, reliable, and consistent data capture

• Generate clear, structured reports and insights

• Maintain system effectiveness and address configuration issues

• Operate independently across both setup and ongoing management



Required Experience

• Strong hands-on experience with Teramind (UAM, DLP, or related modules)

• Proven ability to manage Teramind end-to-end without supervision

• Experience configuring policies and monitoring user activity

• Strong understanding of Teramind architecture, agents, and deployment models

• Ability to diagnose and resolve platform or configuration issues

• Experience producing meaningful and accurate monitoring reports



Working Arrangement

• Remote

• Contractor engagement

• Ongoing long-term work with a consistent pipeline

• Flexible hours with partial alignment to AEST business hours



Application Requirements

To apply, include the following:

1. Teramind Experience

• Outline your direct experience with Teramind

• Specify modules, environments, and scale of deployment

2. Policy Configuration Approach

• How you design and implement monitoring policies

• How you ensure policies are effective and aligned to requirements

3. Reporting and Analysis

• How you analyse user activity

• How you structure and present reports

4. Example Work

• Brief examples of previous Teramind deployments or configurations

• Types of monitoring or reporting you have implemented



This role requires a high level of autonomy, accuracy, and technical competence. If you are confident managing Teramind in a production environment and can deliver reliable outcomes without supervision, we are interested in hearing from you.



Submit your CV and responses for consideration.

title: Video Editor — Escape Agency Burnout • One Brand, World-Class Standards
description:
You’ve done the agency thing. Fast turnarounds, demanding clients, and a culture that rewards hours logged over quality produced. You’re tired.

You want a role where quality actually matters. Where “good enough” isn’t good enough. Where you can slow down enough to do genuinely excellent work — and be part of a team that appreciates it.

This is that role.



THE ROLE

Market Leaders is a lean coaching and marketing education brand. You’ll produce 4–6 long-form videos per month (10–60 minutes each) — YouTube, course content, webinars — with clear briefs, direct creative collaboration, and enough time to do it properly. Hormozi-level quality standards. Peaceful & Profitable culture. No 11 PM revision requests.



WHAT YOU BRING

• 2+ years of long-form video editing — agency or in-house

• Mastery of at least one professional editing tool

• Strong audio mixing — dialogue, music, noise reduction

• Ability to handle consistent volume without sacrificing quality

• Collaborative, proactive, and low-drama



NICE TO HAVE (not required)

•      Familiarity/background of Mandarin language



WHAT YOU GET

• SGD $1,000–1,200/month • Remote, flexible

• No micromanagement, no impossible timelines

• Quarterly paid team retreats

• A team that values craft over speed



A NOTE ON CULTURE

We have a strict no brilliant jerks policy. Skills matter deeply here — so does how you show up for the people around you.

You’re our kind of person if you deliver excellent work without creating friction, take ownership without needing applause, and genuinely celebrate when a teammate wins.

High Performance. Zero Drama. Peaceful & Profitable — we mean all three.



High standards, sustainable pace. This is your sanctuary from agency burnout.

?

APPLY HERE:

title: Social Media and eCommerce Manager
description:
About Us

We’re a growing photography and printing business based in Australia, helping people capture, create, and preserve their most meaningful 
nts. From cameras to premium photo prints and memory restoration, everything we do is about helping families live fully and remember what matters.



Role Overview

We’re looking for a creative and detail-oriented Social Media, Marketing & eCommerce Manager to support our online growth.



This role is focused on content creation, product uploads, SEO, and ongoing website improvements — helping turn visitors into customers and keeping our online store active, engaging, and easy to use.



You’ll be working closely with our team to execute ideas, not just plan them.



Key Responsibilities

Social Media & Content Creation

• Create short-form video content (Reels/TikToks) from provided footage and ideas

• Write captions aligned with our brand voice (warm, family-focused, natural)

• Schedule and publish posts across Instagram,
, and other platforms

• Repurpose content into multiple formats (reels, stories, posts)

• Stay on top of trends and suggest simple, realistic content ideas



eCommerce Management (Shopify)

• Upload new products to our website (images, descriptions, pricing)

• Format product pages for clarity and conversion

• Organise collections and ensure products are easy to find

• Maintain consistency across product listings

• Upload Supplier Promotions to the website



SEO & Website Optimisation

• Write and optimise product descriptions and meta data (SEO-focused)

• Improve page structure and internal linking

• Assist with blog uploads and formatting

• Monitor and improve basic SEO performance



Google & Local Marketing

• Create and schedule Google Business posts

• Update business listings and ensure accuracy

• Support basic local SEO improvements



General Marketing Support

• Assist with campaign setup (e.g. Mother’s Day, Black Friday)

• Help prepare website banners and promotional content

• Suggest improvements to increase engagement and conversions



Requirements

• Experience with Shopify (essential)

• Strong understanding of Instagram Reels / short-form content

• Basic knowledge of SEO (product pages, meta titles/descriptions)

• Good written English (clear, natural tone — not overly corporate)

• Ability to follow systems and execute consistently

• Strong attention to detail



Bonus (Nice to Have)

• Experience in photography, cameras, or creative industries

• Familiarity with Canva, CapCut, or Adobe tools

• Basic understanding of eCommerce conversion strategies



Working Setup

• Remote

• Full Time - must be available for daily checkins

• Must be available for some overlap with Australian business hours



What Success Looks Like

• Consistent flow of engaging content being posted

• New products uploaded accurately and efficiently

• Website feels active, organised, and easy to browse

• Gradual improvement in traffic, engagement, and conversions1. Reel Creation (Core Skill Test)



Application Task (Required)

To be considered for this role, please complete the task below. This helps us understand how you think, create, and execute. Please use our current social posts to understand our branding and tone of voice

Task Overview

You will complete 3 small tasks based on real work you’ll be doing in this role.



Task 1

Create a short-form video (15–30 seconds) using any footage you have (your own or stock).

Theme:

“Why printing your photos matters”

Requirements:

• Add on-screen text/hooks

• Include a clear message/story

• Use captions (subtitles)

• Make it feel natural and engaging (not overly corporate)

Submit as a video file or link



Task 2

Instagram Post (Content + Conversion Test)

Create an Instagram post to promote:

Product: Acrylic Photo Block

Include:

• Image or graphic (can be simple or mockup)

• Caption (engaging + sales-focused)

• Clear hook in the first line

• Call to action

• Optional hashtags

Submit as an image + caption



Task 3

Google Post (Local Marketing Test)

Write a Google Business post for:

Theme: Mother’s Day Photo Gifts

Include:

• Short engaging text (100–150 words)

• Call to action

• Optional emoji use (keep it natural)



What We’re Looking For

• Clear, simple communication (not overcomplicated)

• Understanding of what makes people click, buy, or engage

• Attention to detail

• Ability to execute — not just plan



Important Notes

• Keep it simple — we value clarity over perfection

• Do not use overly generic AI-generated content

• Show us how you think??



Please send your application, along with the completed tasks, to

title: Citation Builder & SEO Reporting Specialist
description:
Company: Apex Growth

Location: Remote

Type: Part-Time (20–30 hours per week, with growth potential)



About Us



Apex Growth is a fast-growing digital marketing agency specializing in Google Maps (Google Business Profile), Apple Maps, and local SEO for smoke shops, liquor stores, and local businesses across the United States.



Our focus is simple:

 • Improve local rankings

 • Increase visibility

 • Drive more customers to our clients



We are looking for a reliable, detail-oriented tea
mber to support citation building and SEO reporting across all accounts.





Role Overview



This is a precision-focused execution role.



Your responsibility is to:

Ensure client business information is accurate across the web and track SEO performance consistently.



You will be working across multiple accounts using structured systems. Accuracy and consistency are critical.





Core Responsibilities



1. Citation Building (Primary Responsibility)

 • Submit business listings to directories

 • Fix NAP (Name, Address, Phone) inconsistencies

 • Ensure all listings match the client’s Google Business Profile exactly

 • Use tools like Search Atlas to identify issues

 • Improve citation scores over time



This work requires strong attention to detail. Small mistakes matter.





2. SEO Reporting

 • Create one report per client each month

 • If managing ~150 clients, this will be approximately 38 reports per week

 • All reporting will be done using Search Atlas (this is the only tool you will use for reports)



Each report should include:

 • Heatmap ranking performance

 • Keyword visibility

 • Progress or changes over time



Reports should be clear, simple, and accurate.





3. Performance Monitoring

 • Identify clients that are not improving or are losing rankings

 • Flag issues immediately



If a client’s rankings worsen:

 • Notify the SEO team in Slack (#seo channel)

 • Clearly explain what changed

 • Work with the team to help resolve the issue and get performance trending upward again





4. Data Accuracy and Quality Control

 • Ensure all information submitted is correct and consistent

 • Double-check all listings and reports

 • Maintain clean, organized data across all clients





Requirements

 • Strong attention to detail

 • Ability to follow instructions exactly

 • Reliable and consistent work habits

 • Comfortable with repetitive tasks

 • Clear written English





Nice to Have

 • Experience with Google Business Profile

 • Experience with local SEO

 • Familiarity with citation building or SEO tools





Compensation

 • Starting at $5/hour

 • Opportunity to increase to $6–$7/hour based on performance

 • Opportunity to grow into a larger role over time





How to Apply



Please include:

 1. A short introduction

 2. Any relevant experience

 3. Your weekly availability

 4. Answer this question:



What is NAP consistency and why is it important for local SEO?





Important



This role requires precision and consistency.



If you do your job well:

 • Clients will perform better

 • The team will rely on your work

 • You will grow quickly within the company

title: Local SEO Professional (Local Heat Map Ranking Specialist)
description:
Local SEO Professional (Local Heat Map Ranking Specialist)



Remote | Part-Time or Full-Time

Apex Growth



Apex Growth is hiring a Local SEO Professional who understands how to rank storefronts on Google Business Profiles and boost visibility across entire local heat maps as far as 18 miles away. If you can consistently move businesses to the top of grid rankings and improve local authority fast, you’re exactly who we want.



Role Responsibilities



You will manage Local SEO for multiple clients (smoke shops, vape shops, barbershops, hair salons, etc.). Your goal is simple: grow rankings, expand visibility, and improve heat maps every single week.



? Google Business Profile Optimization



Complete GBP optimization from categories to services, products, menus, and attributes



Audit and correct NAP issues



Fix duplicate listings



Optimize descriptions, metadata, and geo-tagged images



Build out service area pages on GBP and Websites for stronger relevance



? Local SEO Heat Map Improvement



Run weekly heat maps using SearchAtlas 



Create plans to improve weak grid points



Track and study competitor positioning



Provide ranking improvement reports weekly



Before/after heat maps are preferred but not required



? SearchAtlas Work (Highly Preferred)



Experience with SearchAtlas is highly preferred



If you’ve never used it, you must study and learn it before starting



We will show you how to set up automated GBP posting



Must know how to use or learn:



Automated GBP posts



Automated AI review responses



Q&A Creation & Posting



Heat map tracking



Citation tools



? Citation Building & Local Authority



Build high-quality citations



Clean and correct NAP data



Suppress duplicates



Submit to niche + national directories



? Q&A Creation (Mandatory)



Must create high-quality GBP Questions & Answers weekly or monthly



Must understand how to:



Target keywords in Q&A



Cover products, services, policies, delivery, FAQs



Increase local relevance through well-structured Q&A



? Reputation & Review Optimization



Improve review velocity using optimized templates



Respond to reviews using SEO-rich formats



Set up automated review responses via SearchAtlas



? Content & On-Page Local SEO



Optimize location pages (H-tags, meta, alt text, schema, internal linking)



Post blogs targeting city-level, product-level, and “near me” keywords



Requirements



Strong understanding of Google’s local ranking factors



Experience improving local rankings, especially in competitive niches



Ability to manage multiple clients with consistent weekly progress



Able to work independently and deliver real results



SearchAtlas experience is highly preferred — otherwise must learn it before starting



Bonus (not required): Before/after heat map portfolio



Compensation:



$6 per hour OR you may submit your expected monthly rate - Rate is negotiable for candidates with strong heat map ranking experience



How to Apply



Send:



Any before/after heat maps (if available)



A short outline of your Local SEO process



Portfolio Of Prior experience



Tools you’re experienced with (must be willing to learn SearchAtlas)

title: Outbound Sales | Speaking Engagement Booking Agent
description:
I am an author and speaker who shares my story as a survivor of human trafficking to inspire healing and personal transformation in professionals, and communities.



My work has reached colleges, universities, and organizations where real conversations are being had. I’m expanding into more corporate spaces, and I’m looking for an outbound sales agent who can confidently secure high-value speaking engagements ($25K+).



Your Role: 



 • Identify and connect with decision-makers (HR leaders, event directors, university coordinators)



 • Lead strategic cold outreach 
, LinkedIn)



 • Position speaking engagements in a way that resonates with the organization’s goals and creates demand.



 • Stay consistent with follow-up and relationship-building.



 • Negotiate and close deals that reflect the value of the work. 



Compensation



 • Base pay + commission



 • Commission tied to closed deals



 • Strong earning potential for the right person



This Role Is For You If You:



 • Have experience closing $25K+ deals 

 • Know how to turn cold outreach into conversations and opportunities

 • Are comfortable communicating with decision-makers and leadership

 • Understand how to present value confidently

 • Want your work to be tied to both impact and income



Skillset



1. Strong communication skills – you know how to connect

2. Consistency in follow-up – you don’t let opportunities fall through

3. Emotional awareness – you can represent a powerful story with respect and care 

4. Self-leadership – you take initiative and move without needing constant direction



What Success Looks Like:



 • A consistent pipeline of corporate and university opportunities

 • Signed contracts for booked speaking engagements at the $25K+ level

 • A clear role in growing both the message and the business



Before You Apply:



This role focuses on creating and securing opportunities through outbound outreach.

You’ll have the space to lead, test, and refine your approach.



To Apply:



Please answer the following questions before you apply:



Tell me about a time you created an opportunity from nothing and turned it into a closed deal. What specific steps did you take, and what was the outcome?



What would it mean for you to work with a survivor of human trafficking?

title: Mid/Snjr Visual Designer / Graphic Designer (SaaS)
description:
About the Role



This is a hands-on design role sitting within a fast-moving B2B SaaS marketing team. You’ll be across everything from campaign creatives and social content to EDMs and brand assets — producing high-quality work at pace, across multiple formats and channels.



What You’ll Be Doing



Content Production (Approx. 80%)



- Social and paid ad creatives (Meta, LinkedIn, and others)

- Campaign rollouts and design variations across the funnel

- Collaborating with the growth team to bring strategy to life visually

- High-volume output without compromising on quality or intentionality

- Social media content (LinkedIn, Instagram,
)

- EDM design (HubSpot)

- Marketing collateral — one-pagers, brochures, sales enablement assets

- Supporting blog and visual content creation



Website & Landing Pages (Approx. 10%) 



- Landing page layouts and updates

- Website content refinements and visual improvements

- Basic UX thinking applied to conversion-focused pages



Brand & Identity (Approx. 10%) 



- Contributing to visual identity and brand consistency

- Supporting brand guidelines, templates, and rollout



About You



You’re a junior-to-mid-weight designer who:



- Has a strong foundation in design principles — hierarchy, layout, typography, clarity

- Can work at pace without letting quality slip

- Brings ideas to the table, not just execution

- Is comfortable switching between formats and briefs

- Enjoys working collaboratively and communicating openly



You have a genuine interest in how design connects to marketing and business outcomes. You’re curious about what makes content perform — not just what makes it look good.



We're a fully remote team based in Australia (with tea
mbers across APAC) running a marketing SaaS product.



Skills & Experience



- Experience across graphic and digital design, ideally in a SaaS, startup, marketing or agency environment

- Strong proficiency in Figma and/or Adobe Creative Suite (InDesign, Illustrator, Photoshop) 

- Confidence using Canva for fast-paced content production

- Ability to produce work that is consistent, on-brand, and intentional across digital formats

- Strong communication skills and comfort working in a collaborative team

- Tertiary qualification in communication design or a related field (or equivalent experience)



Desirable (Not Essential)



- EDM platform experience (HubSpot or equivalent)

- CMS experience (we use Webflow but also like WordPress, Squarespace)

- UI/UX tools (Figma)

- Basic understanding of web structure and layout

- Familiarity with AI tools in design workflows



TO APPLY

**Please include a link to your portfolio or other design folio as part of your application. If you do not have one included we will not review the application**.

mbers across APAC) running a marketing SaaS product.

TO APPLY

title: Full-Time Short-Form Video Editor (Instagram Reels & YouTube Shorts)
description:
I’m hiring a full-time video editor to help me produce a high volume of short-form travel content for Instagram (with some YouTube Shorts).



This is a fast-paced, long-term role for someone who understands how to make content that people actually watch all the way through.



About the Content



I create travel content focused on flights, deals, and behind-the-scenes experiences. The style is a mix of:



Vlog-style clips

Voiceover-driven videos (no talking head)

Occasional direct-to-camera/talking head



The goal is not just aesthetic travel content — it’s engaging, informative, and personality-driven.



What This Role Looks Like

Editing multiple reels per day (this is high volume)

Turning raw footage + loose ideas into polished 15–30 second videos

Occasionally editing longer Shorts (up to ~3 minutes for flight reviews)

Working quickly and iterating fast



What You’ll Do

Cut footage down aggressively (remove pauses, filler, dead space)

Maintain fast pacing that keeps retention high

Add high-quality captions and text overlays that grab attention and are on-brand

Shape strong hooks and structure (not just assemble clips)

Use green screen editing when needed

Work with my real voice for voiceovers (no AI voiceovers)

Follow and apply my brand colors and visual style





You’re a great fit if you:



Have experience editing for Reels/TikTok/Shorts

Know how to make videos feel fast and engaging

Are highly skilled with text-on-screen 

Can take a rough idea and make it better

Can handle high output without sacrificing quality

Are responsive and can turn around edits quickly



Bonus Skills (Strong Plus)

Content ideation and brainstorming

Ability to research trends and adapt them to my niche

Understanding of hooks, retention, and what makes content perform



Important Notes

All videos are vertical (9:16)

Captions are required on all videos

Must follow my branding (colors, style, tone)

No AI voiceovers — only my real voice

This role requires speed, taste, and attention to detail



Hiring Process

I will be hiring multiple editors for a paid test project.

From there, I will select one or more editors to work with long-term.



To Apply



Please include:



Examples of short-form videos you’ve edited (Reels/TikTok/Shorts)

A quick note on your editing style and strengths

Your turnaround time for short-form videos
salary: $4 to $10 per hour depending on experien

title: COMMERCIAL LENDING ADMIN VA
description:
JOB TITLE

COMMERCIAL LENDING ADMIN VA

===========================



JOB DESCRIPTION

We are an Australian commercial funding business based on the east coast of Australia. We match borrowers - builders, developers, landowners, and commercial property owners - with private investors and institutional lenders. We are hiring a full-time Commercial Lending Admin VA to take over the administrative side of our operations.



Your day-to-day work will involve setting up and managing document data rooms in Google Drive for each deal, collecting and organising all required documents from clients, following up when documents are overdue, managing diaries, and handling general admin tasks across all active deals. You will be building the filing and document management system from scratch - there is no existing CRM or structured process in place.



If you have experience in finance administration, credit file processing, loan administration, compliance document management, or similar roles where you were responsible for collecting, organising, and tracking documents across multiple matters or cases, we want to hear from you.



---



WHAT WE OFFER



* Stable, long-term role with an established Australian business

* Full-time, 40 hours per week

* PHP 30,000 starting salary during probation

* PHP 33,500 + PHP 1,500 benefits post probation (approximately 1 month)

* 13th month pay

* Government-mandated benefits

* Fully remote - work from home



---



WHAT YOU'LL DO



* Create and manage Google Drive data rooms (shared folders) for each deal - labelling and organising documents by type such as valuations, assets and liabilities statements, contracts, and title documents

* Collect all required documents from clients for each active deal

* Follow up with clients who have not provided documents on time via
 or phone

* Track what has been received and what is still outstanding across all deals

* Handle day-to-day admin tasks assigned by both principals

* Organise files,
s, and deal records

* Manage calendars, schedule meetings, and coordinate follow-ups



---



REQUIREMENTS



* Understanding of finance and basic credit files - terms like valuation, assets and liabilities, settlement, and security should be familiar to you. Prior experience in finance, credit, or lending admin is strongly preferred

* Strong document management and organisational skills - you will be building the filing system from scratch across multiple simultaneous deals

* Google Drive proficiency - comfortable creating shared folders, managing permissions, and maintaining clean file structures

* Proactive follow-up habits - comfortable chasing clients for outstanding documents without waiting to be asked

* Professional written and verbal communication - you will have direct client contact for follow-ups and admin tasks

* Ability to manage competing priorities from two managers without dropping things

* High attention to detail and personal standards - messy or incomplete work will not be accepted

* Reliable computer and stable internet connection



---



NICE TO HAVE



* Prior experience in Australian commercial lending, broking, or finance administration

* Experience setting up document management systems or data rooms from scratch

* Calendar management and executive admin support experience



---



SCHEDULE



* Full-time, 40 hours per week

* Monday to Friday, 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM

* Australian timezone (AEST/AEDT)



---



Once your application is submitted, you will receive an
 from us with the next steps and instructions.

title: Head of E-commerce & Operations – High Fashion Brand (Shopify + ERP + AI Creative) Full-Time
description:
## About the Role

We are a rising high-fashion label with a strong luxury DNA (Ibiza & Palma). We are looking for a driven, creative and ambitious young professional to take full ownership of our e-commerce universe.



This is a true high-fashion role: you will shape the digital presence of a luxury brand, create editorial-level visuals, build exclusive concierge networks and drive growth with precision and style. Not a freelance gig, not a side job — this is your chance to become the central force behind a fashion brand on the rise.



## Key Requirements (Mandatory)

- Full-time dedication (8 hours per day) with real passion for high fashion  

- No other jobs, freelance clients or parallel activities — we want your full creative energy  

- Direct reporting to the founders  

- Immediate real-time responsiveness during working hours  

- Occasional weekend availability when campaigns or drops demand it  



As the brand grows, you will build and lead your own team of creatives and operators.



## Core Responsibilities



### 1. Shopify Management & Luxury Sales Optimization

- End-to-end Shopify management (online + POS)  

- Create high-end product pages with luxury copywriting and visual storytelling  

- Merchandising, upsell/cross-sell and conversion optimization at the highest level  



### 2. High-Fashion Campaigns & Growth

- Build and execute campaigns for Ibiza, Palma and our exclusive concierge channels  

- Design dedicated landing pages that feel like editorial fashion stories  



### 3. Concierge Network Development (Key Luxury Channel)

- Identify and onboard top concierges in hotels, yachts, private clubs and VIP services  

- Create personalized luxury landing pages and tracking systems  

- Grow this high-touch revenue channel within the most exclusive circles  



### 4. AI-Driven Content Creation & Editorial Visuals

- Generate stunning high-fashion lookbooks and model visuals with AI  

- Create packshots, short-form video content and luxury mood imagery  

- Edit with Canva / Photoshop and add sophisticated fashion-tone overlays  



### 5. ERP & Shopify Synchronization (Critical)

- Daily stock control and instant resolution of discrepancies  

- Seamless flow: 3PL ? Ibiza ? Palma stores  



### 6. Operations, Logistics & Customer Experience

- Daily order flow, shipping and returns with brand-aligned luxury service  



### 7. Performance & Reporting

- Weekly high-level sales & channel reports  

- Data-driven action plans to elevate the brand  



## Who We Are Looking For – Strong Young Women with Fashion Culture

- You live and breathe high fashion and luxury culture  

- Proven Shopify expertise + solid ERP experience (mandatory)  

- Sharp operational mind combined with strong creative and commercial flair  

- Fast, precise and highly responsive — you get things done beautifully  

- You have an eye for aesthetics and understand what makes a fashion brand desirable  



## Required Tools

- Shopify (expert level)  

- ERP systems  

- Canva / Photoshop  

- AI tools (Midjourney, Runway, etc.)  

- Landing page builders (Pagefly, Zeno or similar)  



## What We Do NOT Accept

- Freelancers with multiple clients  

- Candidates with other jobs or side activities  

- Slow or unresponsive profiles  

- Pure technicians without fashion feeling  



## Compensation

Competitive full-time base salary + **generous commission** on e-commerce sales and concierge network performance.  

Top performers have the real potential to earn **a lot** — your income grows directly with the brand’s success and has no upper limit!



## Application Process

Only candidates who complete the mandatory test will be considered:

1. Product page creation (high-fashion style)  

2. AI-generated luxury lookbook visual  

3. Campaign landing page structure  

4. ERP discrepancy scenario  



If you are a strong, ambitious young woman with a deep love for fashion culture and you want to build something iconic while earning very well — this is your role.

title: Senior Creator Support Specialist
description:
1stCollab

Senior Creator Support Specialist

Location: Remote – Philippines | Type: Full-Time | Department: Creator Operations



ABOUT 1STCOLLAB



At 1stCollab, content creators — on TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, and LinkedIn — are at the heart of what we do. Every week, we help hundreds of creators find sponsorships with brands like Notion, Character AI, and Framer. To do this, we're in direct contact with thousands of creators on a regular basis, guiding them from the 
nt we reach out about a potential partnership all the way through to content going live and payment in hand.



We're a fast-growing, creator-first platform built to connect brands with creators at scale. We move fast, iterate often, and put our creators at the center of everything we build. Our Creator Support team is a critical part of that experience — you're not just answering messages, you're the voice of the platform and the bridge between our creators and our team.





ROLE OVERVIEW



We're looking for a Senior Creator Support Specialist to join our growing operations team. This is a high-impact, fully remote role based in the Philippines. As a Creator Support tea
mber, you'll be responsible for communicating directly with creators — guiding them through the process of applying for brand campaigns, clarifying partnership details, and providing hands-on support as they produce and post their content.



It's a fun and genuinely unique opportunity. These creators are like mini-celebrities — many have hundreds of thousands, even millions, of followers — and you'll be talking to them directly. It's the kind of role where you'll have a great story to tell at the end of every week.



This is not an entry-level role. We're looking for someone with senior-level experience who can hit the ground running, navigate a fast-moving product environment, and represent 1stCollab with professionalism, warmth, and clarity.





WHAT YOU'LL DO



• Own high-quality, written support across both
 and 1stCollab's built-in platfor
ssaging — responding to creators with clarity, speed, and a natural, human tone

• Communicate directly with creators throughout the full partnership lifecycle: from initial outreach about a brand sponsorship, through content production, posting, and final payment

• Guide creators through the campaign application process, clarifying partnership details and keeping them informed every step of the way

• Provide hands-on support and guidance as creators produce and post their sponsored content

• Adapt quickly to ongoing product updates and process changes — at a startup like ours, the product evolves constantly, and you'll be expected to stay current and communicate changes confidently

• Collaborate with internal teams (operations, product, engineering) to escalate and resolve complex creator issues

• Maintain detailed and accurate records in our CRM, tracking creator interactions, feedback trends, and resolution outcomes

• Communicate with tea
mbers, management, and leadership across all levels with professionalism and clarity

• Contribute to internal documentation, support playbooks, and knowledge bases as the product grows

• Identify recurring patterns in creator feedback and proactively surface them to the broader team





WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Communication & Tone

• Exceptional written English — you write with confidence, clarity, and warmth in both
 and real-time chat environments

• You communicate like a real person, not a support script — our creators should feel like they're talking to someone who genuinely gets them, not an autoresponder

• Experience corresponding with a range of stakeholders — from creators to management and C-level contacts — adjusting tone and depth accordingly



Adaptability & Startup Mindset

• Demonstrated experience working in environments where products, processes, and priorities shift frequently — you don't just tolerate change, you thrive in it

• Ability to maintain quality and efficiency even when workflows or tooling are updated mid-stream

• A proactive, problem-solving mentality — you're the type to find a workaround before waiting to be told one exists



Technical Proficiency

• Solid working knowledge of CRM platforms (e.g., HubSpot, Zendesk, Salesforce, Intercom, or similar)

• Comfortable with technical tools and digital workflows — you can learn new platforms quickly without hand-holding

• Familiar with structured support systems, ticketing tools, tagging/routing, and basic reporting



Remote Work & Team Fit

• Proven track record of working successfully in a fully remote environment — you're self-managed, reliable, and communicative

• A stable, high-speed internet connection is required — this is non-negotiable for a remote support role

• You're the kind of teammate people actually enjoy working with. We value kindness, low ego, and genuine collaboration

• Flexible and available to support a schedule that aligns with our users' needs (US business hours or overlapping availability)





MUST-HAVES



• 2–3+ years of customer or creator support experience at a senior or specialist level

• Experience in fast-changing or high-growth environments where priorities shift quickly

• Stable, high-speed internet connection with a reliable remote workspace

• Schedule flexibility to support cross-timezone collaboration

• Proven ability to work well independently and as part of a remote team — references that speak to your work ethic and team presence

• Strong tracker management skills — able to build and maintain organized trackers with charts, conditional formatting, and dashboard-level visibility for the broader team

• Familiarity with Notion for documentation, project tracking, and team collaboration

• Proficient and proactive use of AI tools to improve efficiency and quality of work

• Comfortable communicating with third-party support teams (e.g., Stripe) via call when needed to resolve creator payment issues post-campaign





NICE-TO-HAVES



• Experience working with a US-based or globally distributed tech company

• Familiarity with startup culture and the pace that comes with it

• Proficiency with Slack and Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, Gmail)

• Hands-on experience using AI tools in a professional setting

• Background or interest in influencer marketing, creator economy, or digital advertising

• Mid-to-senior level team lead or mentorship experience — a plus as our team scales





WHY 1STCOLLAB



• Work with a collaborative, low-bureaucracy team that respects your expertise and autonomy

• Be part of a growing platform at an exciting inflection point — your work will have real impact

• Fully remote — work from anywhere in the Philippines with the flexibility to do your best work

• A team culture built on kindness, transparency, and continuous improvement

title: Affiliate & Influencer Manager Support
description:
The role

Half the role is keeping operations tight. The other half is helping us grow.



We're a fast-moving affiliate team that's expanding into influencer and creator outreach. AI handles the heavy reporting — so your job isn't to pull numbers. It's to read them, act on them, and make sure nothing falls through the cracks while we scale.

You'll work directly with the affiliate manager and founder. The work is hands-on, the feedback is fast, and what you do shows up in revenue.



What you'll own

Affiliate operations (core admin) Manage affiliate requests — links, creatives, access. QA tracking, funnels, and offers to make sure everything works before and during launches. Run daily operations so the program doesn't miss a beat. This is the foundation. Get this right and everything else gets easier.



Influencer & creator outreach (new + growing) Source and reach out to relevant creators and influencers to bring into the program. Build and manage an outreach pipeline — track who's been contacted, who's responded, who's live. Follow up consistently and help onboard new partners with the right assets and information. You're building relationships that directly grow our reach.



Performance monitoring (AI-assisted) Our AI surfaces the data. Your job is to interpret it — catch a drop in EPC before it becomes a problem, flag an affiliate who's quietly scaling, identify what's underperforming and ask why. Then tell someone. You don't just read reports. You form a point of view.



Affiliate communication & follow-ups Push top performers to scale. Re-engage dormant affiliates. Make sure active partners always have what they need. You're the connective tissue between the program and the people running it.

Growth support & insights Spot a creative that's fatiguing and suggest a new angle. Flag a reactivation opportunity. Notice what's working and push to do more of it. Small observations compound into real revenue. You're expected to bring ideas, not just complete tasks.



TikTok & creator support (secondary) Track trending creatives, help source and test new creators, keep growth experiments organized. Not the main act — but a fast-growing one.



What we're looking for

High ownership and initiative

Sharp attention to detail

Clear communicator

Organized under pressure

Can form insights from data

Comfortable with outreach

Bonus: Experience with ClickBank, Digistore24, performance funnels, or TikTok/influencer workflows — but we'll back the right person regardless.



This role is not for you if...

You want a checklist, not a challenge

You treat "support" as code for "just follow instructions"

You shut down when the process isn't fully mapped out

You're not motivated by having skin in the game



What you get

A real seat at the table — with pay that reflects how well you perform. You'll see the numbers, influence decisions, and grow with the program. The base gets you stable. The bonus rewards you for actually moving the needle. And as the program scales, so does your ceiling.



How to apply

Send your application to 
 with the subject line: I'm interested

Please include your resume and a short note on your background and why this role caught your attention
